title: [FIRRTL][circt-reduce] module-name-sanitizer can't handle FIRRTL classes
body:
Consider the following:

``` mlir
firrtl.circuit "Foo" {
  firrtl.class @Bar() {
  }
  firrtl.module @Foo() {
    %CabooseTileParams = firrtl.object @Bar()
  }
}
```

This crashes if run with:

``` console
# circt-reduce -skip-initial -test='exit 0' module-name-sanitizer.0.mlir -include module-name-sanitizer -o /dev/null
Reading input
Testing input with `exit 0`
Initial module has size 153
Trying reduction `module-name-sanitizer`
PLEASE submit a bug report to https://github.com/llvm/circt and include the crash backtrace.
Stack dump:
0.	Program arguments: circt-reduce -skip-initial "-test=exit 0" module-name-sanitizer.0.mlir -include module-name-sanitizer -o /dev/null
Stack dump without symbol names (ensure you have llvm-symbolizer in your PATH or set the environment var `LLVM_SYMBOLIZER_PATH` to point to it):
0  circt-reduce             0x000000010421fc34 llvm::sys::PrintStackTrace(llvm::raw_ostream&, int) + 56
1  circt-reduce             0x000000010421db1c llvm::sys::RunSignalHandlers() + 172
2  circt-reduce             0x00000001042206dc SignalHandler(int, __siginfo*, void*) + 300
3  libsystem_platform.dylib 0x000000018fd516a4 _sigtramp + 56
4  circt-reduce             0x000000010460aa4c circt::firrtl::InstanceOp::setModuleName(llvm::StringRef) + 40
5  circt-reduce             0x000000010515f050 (anonymous namespace)::ModuleNameSanitizer::rewrite(circt::firrtl::CircuitOp) + 1320
6  circt-reduce             0x00000001041ab0dc execute(mlir::MLIRContext&) + 3988
7  circt-reduce             0x00000001041a9ebc main + 300
8  dyld                     0x000000018f976b98 start + 6076
zsh: segmentation fault  circt-reduce -skip-initial -test='exit 0' module-name-sanitizer.0.mlir   -o 
```
